Value: In archives, metadata is used to create consistent and structured descriptions of resources and their characteristics. Metadata is independent and can either be stored together with the resources it describes or separately. Metadata plays a key role in the future of your stored research data: it contains important information which for example enables you and others to search for and find your data.\nA metadata set consists mainly of descriptive categories, the so-called elements and their values. To facilitate communication with other systems, predefined standards are used for the elements and controlled vocabularies for the values.
Suggested Key Value: In archives, metadata is used to create consistent and structured descriptions of resources and their characteristics. Metadata is independent and can either be stored together with the resources it describes or separately. Metadata plays a key role in the future of your stored research data: it contains important information which for example enables you and others to search for and find your data.\nA metadata set consists mainly of descriptive categories, the so-called elements and their values. To facilitate communication with other systems, predefined standards are used for the elements and controlled vocabularies for the values.

Value:
Research data is all digital objects created in the course of academic work, for instance, measurements, surveys, observations, simulations, interviews, and software.
If you still believe you will not generate or reuse any data in your project, we recommend manually filling in the original DMP templates instead of using the DMP tool.
FWF: Please fill in the required administrative information in section I.1 and explain why the project will not produce or use data in section I.2 Data management responsibilities and resources. The rest of the template should be empty.
EU (Horizon Europe): Please fill in the required administrative information on pages 1-3 and explain why the project will not produce or use data in section 1 Data summary. If applicable, enter information in section 3 Other research outputs.
Suggested Key Value:

Value: This is a good time to think about your expectations. You should already have an idea of what kind of (digital) material you will be creating or analysing in your project, even if it is still vague and you don’t have specific details. If this is the case, click on “Specify datasets.” and describe the expected material as well as possible in this early stage. You can mention in the DMP (= your export document from the tool) that the data description is a broad estimation that will be updated in a later version of the DMP. If you come to the conclusion that you will not create or analyse any data, click on “No data will be created or analysed.” and follow the instructions there.

Value: The exported DMP is an automatically created draft that you still need to add the finishing touches to. If you have a choice, we recommend editing in Word. Check the content carefully and complete any missing information, including the DMP version details on the cover sheet. Once ready, you can save your DMP as a PDF and provide it to the funder. Please note that you do not need to transfer your changes back into the DMP tool, as the created DMP document will be the basis for subsequent versions, not the tool.
Suggested Key Value: The exported DMP is an automatically created draft that you still need to add the finishing touches to. If you have a choice, we recommend editing in Word. Check the content carefully and complete any missing information, including the DMP version details on the cover sheet. Once ready, you can save your DMP as a PDF and provide it to the funder. Please note that you do not need to transfer your changes back into the DMP tool, as the created DMP document will be the basis for subsequent versions, not the tool.

Value: A DMP is a living document and it is recommended to keep it up to date – for yourself, the project team, and the funder. The funders usually ask for updates at certain milestones and at the end of the project. Any changes can easily be done within the document, you do not need the tool for updates. Nevertheless, if you wish to add or change details and create a new version in the DMP tool, for example to update the machine-actionable information, remember to carefully check and complete the exported DMP as described above.
Suggested Key Value: A DMP is a living document and it is recommended to keep it up to date – for yourself, the project team, and the funder. The funders usually ask for updates at certain milestones and at the end of the project. Any changes can easily be done within the document, you do not need the tool for updates. Nevertheless, if you wish to add or change details and create a new version in the DMP tool, for example to update the machine-actionable information, remember to carefully check and complete the exported DMP as described above.
